tool.name: GROBID DataStet
tool.description: identify dataset mentions in scientific articles
tool.command:
#if $input.ext == "pdf" and $output_format == "json": #set endpoint = "annotateDatasetPDF" #elif $input.ext == "pdf" and $output_format == "tei": #set endpoint = "processDataseerPDF" #elif $input.ext == "tei" and $output_format == "tei": #set endpoint = "processDataseerTEI" #else: echo 'JSON output is only available for PDF input in DataStet 0.8.1.' >&2 exit 2 #end if
bash '$__tool_directory__/run_datastet_service.sh' --endpoint '$endpoint' --input '$input' --output '$output' $add_paragraph_context

param.name: input
param.label: Scientific article PDF or TEI XML

param.name: add_paragraph_context
param.label: Add paragraph context
param.help: Only used by the PDF annotation endpoint.

tool.help:
**GROBID DataStet** starts the DataStet service inside the Galaxy job and identifies dataset mentions in a scientific article.
PDF input can be processed in two ways. With JSON output, the tool calls ``/service/annotateDatasetPDF`` and returns dataset mentions, coordinates, references, page metadata, and contextual use/create/share scores. With TEI output, the tool calls ``/service/processDataseerPDF`` and returns a TEI document enriched with DataStet annotations.
TEI input is sent to ``/service/processDataseerTEI`` and returns enriched TEI. DataStet 0.8.1 does not expose a public TEI-to-JSON service route, so JSON output is limited to PDF input.
Use the GROBID wrapper first when you want to convert a PDF to TEI before running DataStet explicitly. The service is started and stopped for each Galaxy job; no external DataStet server is required.
